title: The Log Is The Agent - Ishaan Sehgal, Omnara
summary: Ishaan Sehgal argues that agent identity should reside in durable session logs rather than in models, tools, or compute infrastructure. He draws an analogy to video game save files to illustrate how logs enable portability, durability, and continuity across different models and runtimes. He contends that as models commoditize, the differentiator shifts to infrastructure that keeps logs independent from model providers.
writeup: Ishaan Sehgal of Omnara makes the case that an agent's identity lives in its durable session logs, not in the underlying model, tools, or compute. The talk uses a video game save file analogy to show how append-only logs deliver portability, durability, and continuity across different models and runtimes. The central argument: as models commoditize, the differentiator moves to infrastructure that keeps logs independent from any single model provider.
